title: FedRAMP 20x Unified KSI Validations
description: Machine-readable schema for the unified KSI validation results produced by the System 2.5 validation engine. Published to the Trust Center as the master compliance scorecard.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp when this validation run was generated

passed.description: Number of KSIs that passed validation

failed.description: Number of KSIs that failed validation

pass_rate.description: Overall pass rate as a percentage string (e.g. '100.0%')

circuit_breaker.description: Circuit breaker state protecting against cascading failures

failure_threshold.description: Failure rate threshold to trip the breaker

critical_findings.description: Summary of critical security findings detected during validation

ksis_skipped.description: KSI IDs skipped during this validation run (e.g. due to circuit breaker)

assertion_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of the validation outcome

requirement.description: The KSI requirement description being validated

implementation_summary.description: How the control is implemented in production

justification.description: Why this validation approach satisfies the KSI

cli_command.description: Semicolon-delimited CLI commands that were executed

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of this individual validation

score.description: Compliance score (0-100) based on resource-level evaluation

evidence_path.description: Path to the primary evidence artifact for this validation

total_category_controls.description: Total controls in this KSI's category

resources_unverified.description: Number of resources that could not be verified

resource.description: Resource identifier. Typical case: a string (often redacted to a '[resource]' hash). Null is permitted when the finding is engine-side metadata rather than tied to a specific AWS resource (e.g., compliance-artifact freshness checks that key off the artifact filename rather than an ARN). The object form is a known-degraded case where an upstream primitive surfaces the raw response item (e.g., an IdentityStore user record with FamilyName/GivenName); consumers should stringify defensively and the engine should normalize this in a future release.

status.description: Individual resource evaluation result. PASS / FAIL = definitive verdicts; WARNING = degraded but not failing (e.g., 96% rate against 100% target); ACTIVITY = unverified (engine collected the response but the outcome class couldn't assert a definitive verdict — typically a primitive shape it doesn't recognize, or empty data permitted by register policy); UNVERIFIED = legacy alias of ACTIVITY retained for back-compat.

reason.description: Explanation of the resource evaluation outcome

mode.description: Which Mode the engine evaluated this KSI under. 'capability' = Mode 1 (the operational system exists and runs); 'output' = Mode 2 (an output-rate metric meets target). Mode 3 (critical override) applies on top of either mode and forces FAIL regardless; it does not appear here as a distinct value.

capability_status.description: Mode 1 capability verdict. Present for KSIs evaluated under Mode 1 (capability); null for KSIs evaluated purely under Mode 2 (output) where the capability is implicit in the metric source.

display_status.description: Lowercase UI-friendly status. Equivalent to the assertion boolean but in a string form the Trust Center frontend can use directly without case conversion. Always one of 'pass' or 'fail' — ACTIVITY / WARNING per-resource statuses don't propagate up to the KSI display state, only PASS or FAIL do.

commands_executed.description: Total number of CLI commands the engine ran for this KSI.

successful_commands.description: Number of CLI commands that completed without execution failure (network error, AccessDenied, command-not-found, etc.). The ratio successful_commands / commands_executed is a tech-quality indicator independent of the compliance verdict — high command-failure rate may mask the real KSI state (see Tracker #255 P2 cluster pattern).

command_executions.description: Per-command execution metadata. Useful for diagnosing why a KSI returned an unexpected verdict (e.g., one of the commands hit AccessDeniedException). Mirrors what the engine writes to evidence_v2_engine/<KSI>/_execution_log.json on gated branches.

index.description: 0-indexed position within the KSI's cli_commands array.

command.description: The CLI command string as executed (post variable substitution).

description.description: Free-form note from the cli_commands register entry (the 'note' field).

status.description: Whether the command exited cleanly with valid output.

exit_code.description: Process exit code; null when the command never started.

execution_time.description: Wall-clock seconds the command took, or null when not measured.

error_message.description: Captured stderr / exception message when status='failed'; null otherwise.
